---
name: tsdoc-api-docs
description: >
  Adds production-quality TSDoc comments and Swagger/OpenAPI JSDoc blocks to OctoCAT API
  repository and route files. Use this skill when asked to document, add docs, improve
  documentation, or add Swagger/OpenAPI annotations to any file in api/src/repositories/
  or api/src/routes/. Follows the exact TSDoc + Swagger style used in this repo.
  Works across VS Code, Copilot CLI, and Copilot Coding Agent.
argument-hint: "[repository or route file path]"
---

# TSDoc + Swagger API Documentation — OctoCAT API

## When to Use This Skill
- Documenting a repository class or route file
- Asked to "add docs", "document this", "add TSDoc", "add Swagger", or "update OpenAPI"
- Reviewing a file and noticing missing or incomplete documentation

## Reference Files (read these first)
Before writing any documentation, always read:
1. `api/src/repositories/suppliersRepo.ts` — gold standard TSDoc style
2. `api/src/routes/order.ts` — gold standard Swagger JSDoc style
3. `api/api-swagger.json` — existing `$ref` targets for request/response schemas

Study their patterns, then apply the same style to the target file.

## TSDoc Standard (Repository Files)

See [doc-template.md](./doc-template.md) for copy-paste blocks.

Every **public method** must have ALL of these tags:

| Tag | Required | Notes |
|-----|----------|-------|
| One-line summary | Yes | Imperative verb: "Retrieves…", "Inserts…", "Deletes…" |
| `@param` | Yes — one per parameter | Include name and description |
| `@returns` | Yes | Describe type and shape returned |
| `@throws {ErrorClass}` | Yes | One per possible thrown error; use exact class name |
| `@example` | Yes | At least one realistic call + usage of the result |

Error class names (from `src/utils/errors.ts`):
- `NotFoundError` — record not found by ID
- `ValidationError` — invalid input
- `ConflictError` — unique constraint violation
- `DatabaseError` — unexpected SQLite error

## Swagger JSDoc Standard (Route Files)

Every **route handler** must have an `@openapi` JSDoc block above it.

### Required Fields per Endpoint
- `summary` — one-line description (imperative: "List all suppliers")
- `tags` — must match existing tag groups in `api-swagger.json`
- `parameters` — all path and query params with `in:`, `name:`, `required:`, `schema.type:`
- `requestBody` — for `POST` and `PUT`; use `$ref` to existing schemas where available
- `responses` — document **every** possible status code:

| Status | When to include |
|--------|----------------|
| `200` | Successful GET or PUT |
| `201` | Successful POST (created) |
| `400` | Bad request / validation error |
| `404` | Entity not found |
| `409` | Conflict (duplicate unique value) |
| `422` | Unprocessable entity |
| `500` | Internal server error |

## Validation Checklist

Before finishing, verify:
- [ ] Every `@param` has a name and description
- [ ] Every method has at least one `@throws` (or `@throws — never` if truly safe)
- [ ] Every method has at least one `@example`
- [ ] Every route documents ALL possible response codes
- [ ] No `any` types in JSDoc type annotations
- [ ] Swagger tags match existing tags in `api-swagger.json`

## What NOT to Change
- Test files (`*.test.ts`)
- Migration files (`sql/migrations/*.sql`)
- Seed files (`sql/seed/*.sql`)
- `package.json`, `tsconfig.json`, or other config files

## Output Location & Naming

Write the documented version to a **new file** — do not modify the original source file:
```
api/generated/skill-tsdoc-api-docs/{source-basename}.{YYYYMMDD-HHmmss}.documented.ts
```

- `{source-basename}` — name of the source file without extension (e.g. `suppliersRepo` from `suppliersRepo.ts`)
- `{YYYYMMDD-HHmmss}` — current date and time (e.g. `20260302-143000`)

Example: `api/generated/skill-tsdoc-api-docs/suppliersRepo.20260302-143000.documented.ts`

Do **not** modify the original source file. As the **very first line** of the output file, add:

```typescript
// 🤖 Generated by: skill-tsdoc-api-docs | {source-basename}.ts | {YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss}
```

## Optional: Architecture Notes
If the documented method changes observable API behavior, append a brief note to `docs/backend-architecture.md`.
